# pangolin_site (Resource)

Manages a Pangolin site. A site represents a network endpoint — typically a Newt client — that tunnels traffic into your private network.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pangolin_site" "example" {
  org_id  = "your-org-id"
  name    = "example-site"
  newt_id = "r35trvxvi6ivchq"
  secret  = var.site_secret
  address = "10.88.1.0"
  type    = "newt"
}
```

## Import

Sites can be imported using the format `org_id/site_id`:

```shell
terraform import pangolin_site.example your-org-id/42
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the site.
- `newt_id` (String) The Newt client ID for this site.
- `org_id` (String) The ID of the organization this site belongs to.
- `secret` (String, Sensitive) The secret key for the Newt client.
- `type` (String) The site type (e.g. `"newt"`).

### Optional

- `address` (String) The network address assigned to this site.

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The ID of the site.

## Notes

- `newt_id`, `secret`, `type`, and `org_id` are **immutable** — changing any of these attributes forces a new site to be created.
- `secret` is write-only; its value is not returned by the API and is stored only in Terraform state.
